volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: Pseudolus (Section 1.2.200 - Section 1.3.255c)

sections.label: Section 1.2.200
sections.original: dévinxere ad taúrum , item ego te dístringam ad carnárium ;
sections.translation: to the bull , aye , this day as well , will I tie you up to the larder ; that , in fact , shall be your bull .

sections.label: Section 1.2.201b
sections.translation: I ’ m quite enraged by the talk of this fellow ;

sections.label: Section 1.2.202
sections.translation: that we should suffer the youth of Attica to encourage here [*] this fellow ! Where are they — where are they skulking , they of mature age , who have their amorous dealings with this procurer ? Why don ’ t they meet ? Why don ’ t they one and all deliver the public from this pestilence ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.205
sections.original: sed nímium stultus , nímis fui indóctus : illine aúdeant
sections.translation: But I am very simple , and very ignorant ; they would venture , of course , to do that to those , to whom their passions compel them , to their misfortune , to be subservient , and , at the same time , prevent them from doing that against them which they would rather wish to do .

sections.label: Section 1.2.206_207
sections.translation: Hush !

sections.label: Section 1.2.206_207b
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.208
sections.original: Male mórigeru ’ (s e) s mihi , quóm sermoni huius óbsonas .
sections.translation: Pshaw ! you are not very obliging . Why are you drowning his talk [*] by your noise ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.209
sections.translation: I ’ ll be silent .

sections.label: Section 1.2.209b
sections.original: At taceas málo multo quám tacere dicas .
sections.translation: But I ’ d much rather you would be silent , than that you should say you will be silent .

sections.label: Section 1.2.209c
sections.translation: And you ,

sections.label: Section 1.2.210
sections.original: Xýstilis , fac ut ánimum advortas , quóius amatorés olivi
sections.translation: Xystilis , take you care and give me your attention — you whose fanciers have large quantities of oil at home . If oil shall not be brought me here forthwith in leathern bags , I ’ ll to-morrow cause yourself to be carried off in a leathern bag to the prostitutes ’ shambles [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.2.215
sections.original: íbi tibi adeo léctus dabitur , úbi tu hau somnum cápias , sed ubi
sections.translation: There a bed shall be given you , I warrant , where you can have no rest , but where , even to downright fainting — You understand what ’ s the tendency of that which I ’ m saying ? Will you tell me , you viper you , you who have so many of your fanciers so right well laden with their oil ,

sections.label: Section 1.2.219
sections.original: num quoípiam est hodié tua tuorum ópera conservórum
sections.translation: is now the head of any one of your fellow-slaves a bit the better anointed by your means , or do I , myself , get my dainty morsels a bit the better seasoned with oil [*] for it ? But I understand — you don ’ t care much about oil ; with wine you anoint yourself . Only wait a bit ; by my troth I ’ ll punish you for all at one spell , unless indeed this day you contrive to manage all these things that I ’ ve been speaking of .

sections.label: Section 1.2.225
sections.original: tu aútem , quae pro cápite argentum míhi iam iamque sémper numeras ,
sections.translation: But as for you , Phœnicium , I tell you this , you pet of the mighty men — you who have been for so long a time always paying down to me your money for your liberty — you who only know how to promise , but don ’ t know how to pay what you have promised ; unless this day all your keep is brought me here out of the stores of your customers , to-morrow , Phœnicium , with a true Phœnician hide [*] , you ’ ll pay a visit to the strumpets ’ shambles .

sections.label: Section 1.3.230
sections.original: Pseudóle , non audis quae híc loquitur ?
sections.translation: Pseudolus , don ’ t you hear what he says ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.230b
sections.original: Audío , ere , equidem atque animum ádvorto .
sections.translation: I hear it , master , and I give good heed .

sections.label: Section 1.3.231
sections.original: Quid mi és auctor , huic út mittam , ne amícam hic meam prostítuat ?
sections.translation: What do you advise me to send him , that he mayn ’ t devote my mistress to dishonor ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.232
sections.original: Nil cúrassis , liquido és animo : ego pró me et pro te cúrabo .
sections.translation: Don ’ t you trouble yourself about that ; be of cheerful mind . I ’ ll manage for myself and for you . For some time past I ’ ve been on terms of goodwill with him , and he with me ; and our friendship is of old standing . I ’ ll send him this day , on his birthday , a mischief heavy and well-matured .

sections.label: Section 1.3.235
sections.translation: What ’ s the plan ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.235b
sections.translation: Can ’ t you attend to something else ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.235c
sections.translation: But —

sections.label: Section 1.3.235d
sections.translation: Tut .

sections.label: Section 1.3.235e
sections.translation: I ’ m distracted .

sections.label: Section 1.3.235f
sections.translation: Harden your heart .

sections.label: Section 1.3.236
sections.translation: I cannot .

sections.label: Section 1.3.236b
sections.translation: Make yourself to can .

sections.label: Section 1.3.236c
sections.translation: By what means , pray , can I prevail upon my feelings ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.236d
sections.translation: Carry you out that which is to your advantage , rather than give heed with your feelings to the thing that ’ s disadvantageous .

sections.label: Section 1.3.238
sections.original: Nugae ístaec sunt : non iúcundumst nisi amáns facit stulte .
sections.translation: That ’ s nonsense ; there is no pleasure , unless a lover acts like a fool .

sections.label: Section 1.3.238b
sections.translation: Do you persist ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.239
sections.original: O Pseúdole mi , sine sím nihili ,
sections.translation: O my dear Pseudolus , let me be undone — do let me , please .

sections.label: Section 1.3.239c
sections.translation: I ’ ll let you ; only let me go .

sections.label: Section 1.3.240
sections.original: Mane , máne , iam ut voles med ésse ita ero .
sections.translation: Stay , stay . As you shall , then , wish me to be , so will I be .

sections.label: Section 1.3.240b
sections.translation: Now , at last , you are in your senses .

sections.label: Section 1.3.241
sections.translation: The day is passing ; I ’ m causing delay to myself . Boy , do you go before me .

sections.label: Section 1.3.241c
sections.translation: Hallo there ! he ’ s going ; why don ’ t you call him back ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.242
sections.translation: Why in such a hurry ? Gently .

sections.label: Section 1.3.242b
sections.translation: But before he ’ s gone .

sections.label: Section 1.3.242c
sections.original: Quíd hoc , malum , tam plácide is , puere ?
sections.translation: Why the plague do you go so slowly , boy ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.243
sections.original: Hódie nate , heus , hódie nate , tíbi ego dico , heus , hódie nate ,
sections.translation: You born on this day , hallo ! you born on this day ; I ’ m calling to you ; hallo ! you born on this day , come you back and look at us . Although you are busy ,

sections.label: Section 1.3.245
sections.original: morámur . mane , ém conloquí qui volúnt te .
sections.translation: we want you ; stop — it ’ s because some persons want to speak to you .

sections.label: Section 1.3.246
sections.original: Quid hóc est ? quis ést qui morám mi occupáto
sections.translation: What ’ s this ? Who is it , when I ’ m busy , causes me unseasonable delay ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.247b
sections.translation: He that has been your supporter .

sections.label: Section 1.3.248b
sections.original: Mortuóst qui fuít : qui sit úsust .
sections.translation: He ’ s dead that has been ; only he that is , is now alive .

sections.label: Section 1.3.249
sections.translation: You are too saucy .

sections.label: Section 1.3.249b
sections.translation: You are too troublesome .

sections.label: Section 1.3.249c
sections.translation: Seize the fellow : follow him up .

sections.label: Section 1.3.249d
sections.translation: Go on , boy .

sections.label: Section 1.3.250
sections.translation: Let ’ s go and meet him this way .

sections.label: Section 1.3.250b
sections.translation: May Jupiter confound you , whoever you are .

sections.label: Section 1.3.251b
sections.translation: That for yourself I wish .

sections.label: Section 1.3.251c
sections.translation: And for both of you do I . Turn you this way , boy .

sections.label: Section 1.3.252b
sections.translation: May we not speak with you ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.253
sections.translation: Why , it doesn ’ t please me .

sections.label: Section 1.3.253b
sections.translation: But if it ’ s something to your advantage ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.254
sections.original: Licétne , opsecró , bitere án non licet ?
sections.translation: Am I allowed to go away , pray , or am I not ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.254b
sections.translation: Pshaw !

sections.label: Section 1.3.255
sections.translation: Stop .

sections.label: Section 1.3.255b
sections.translation: Let me go .

sections.label: Section 1.3.255c
sections.translation: Ballio , listen .
